evals.name: c-basic-vulnerabilities
evals.prompt: Audit this C file for security vulnerabilities: .claude/skills/security-audit/scripts/test_vulnerable.c
evals.expected_output: Report should identify strcpy, gets, sprintf as HIGH/CRITICAL severity, and suggest secure alternatives

assertions.name: detects_dangerous_gets
assertions.description: Report must mention gets() as CRITICAL or HIGH severity

assertions.name: detects_strcpy_usage
assertions.description: Report must identify strcpy as buffer overflow risk

assertions.name: suggests_secure_alternatives
assertions.description: Report should suggest strncpy, fgets as alternatives

evals.name: c-advanced-vulnerabilities
evals.prompt: Run a comprehensive security audit on this C file and identify all vulnerabilities including integer overflow, array bounds, and strncpy issues: .claude/skills/security-audit/scripts/test_advanced.c
evals.expected_output: Should detect integer overflow in malloc multiplication, array access without bounds check, strncpy without null terminator, and size_t negative assignment

assertions.name: detects_integer_overflow
assertions.description: Should detect unchecked multiplication in malloc

assertions.name: detects_strncpy_null
assertions.description: Should warn about strncpy missing null terminator

evals.name: audit-script-execution
evals.prompt: Run the security audit script on test_advanced.c and show me the results in markdown format
evals.expected_output: Should execute audit.py successfully and generate formatted report with findings

assertions.name: report_has_structure
assertions.description: Output should have proper markdown structure
expected: # C 语言安全审计报告
